subject: Mathematics
title: John Nash: Beautiful and Troubled Mind
description: A micro-lesson that makes John Nash: Beautiful and Troubled Mind usable.

hook.text: John Nash revolutionized economics with a 27-page dissertation—then spent decades battling schizophrenia.

buildup.text: His Nash equilibrium describes situations where no player can gain by changing strategy alone.

discovery.text: Game theory, powered by his work, now shapes auctions, trade deals, and evolutionary biology.

twist.text: He heard voices for 30 years. Colleagues quietly shielded him, giving him a desk at Princeton.

climax.text: He recovered gradually and won the Nobel Prize in Economics in 1994.

quiz.question: What concept did John Nash introduce?
